title: Removing Consecutive Repeated Characters from a String with Edge Cases

statement.markdown:
Given a string s, your task is to remove all consecutive repeated characters from the string. If there are any remaining characters left after removing all consecutive repeated characters, return a new string. If the string is empty, return an empty string.
      

The string contains only lowercase English letters and the string s is not empty.
statement.notes: Time and space complexity: O(n), where n is the length of the string.

examples.explanation: Remove consecutive repeated characters: a, b are consecutive repeated characters, so we remove them.
Remove consecutive repeated characters: a, z are consecutive repeated characters, so we remove them.
Remove consecutive repeated characters: a, b, c are consecutive repeated characters, so we remove them.

hints.text: Check if current character is the same as next character. If yes, skip the next character.

editorial.title: Removing Consecutive Repeated Characters from a String
editorial.intuition: We can use a two-pointer technique to traverse the string and remove consecutive repeated characters.
editorial.bruteForce: We can use a brute force approach to generate all possible substrings and return the longest substring without consecutive repeated characters.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a two-pointer technique to traverse the string and remove consecutive repeated characters.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: This solution correctly removes all consecutive repeated characters from the string.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run to remove consecutive repeated characters: abcxxd -> abcd
commonMistakes: Not checking if the string is empty
